steps.title: Convert slope into a second point
steps.content: Use slope-intercept form $g(x)=mx+b$. The slope is $5$, so $m=5$. The point $(2,47)$ is on the graph.

steps.title: Fit the equation
steps.content: Substitute $(2,47)$ into $g(x)=5x+b$:$$47=5(2)+b$$ $$47=10+b\Rightarrow b=37$$So the equation is $g(x)=5x+37$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: $g(x) = 37x$ has slope $37$, and the remaining alternative swaps the slope and intercept. $g(x) = 5a$ is not the right expression in terms of $x$. $g(x) = 5x + 37$, $g(x)=5x+37$, has slope $5$ and gives $g(2)=47$. $g(x) = 5x + 37$ is correct.
